finding_name: Bash Remote Code Execution via Environment Variable Injection (Shellshock / CVE-2014-6271 / CVE-2014-6277 / CVE-2014-6278)
analyst_note: CVE-2014-6271 / CVE-2014-6277 / CVE-2014-6278 (Shellshock): Remote code execution via crafted environment variables in Bash < 4.3 patch 25. Exploitable through CGI scripts, SSH ForceCommand, DHCP clients, and other bash-invocation vectors. The nmap http-shellshock script uses a safe echo test: 'Cookie: () { :;}; echo; echo shellshock'. If /cgi-bin/ is accessible, recommend the nmap script as a safe PoC test. This affects any server running CGI scripts via bash. Fixed: Bash 4.3 patch 25 (OS vendor patches required — check distro security advisories).
